channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: A DOGE employee is put in charge of the US Institute of Peace, a court filing alleges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An official allied with Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency has been put in charge of the U.S. Institute of Peace, a congressionally created and funded think tank targeted by President Donald Trump for closure, according to a federal court filing Monday.
item.dc:creator: GARY FIELDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration says it deported 17 more ‘violent criminals’ to El Salvador
item.description: The Trump administration says it has deported 17 more “violent criminals” from the Tren de Aragua and MS-13 gangs to El Salvador, as it doubles down on a policy of removing people from the U.S. to third countries despite criticism over lack of transparency and human rights protections.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Santana And Marcos Aleman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump to sign executive order to protect Americans from 'exploitive ticket scalping' in entertainment industry
item.description: President Trump is expected to sign an executive order to protect Americans from “exploitive ticket scalping" in the concert and entertainment industry, Fox News Digital has learned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump keeps talking about running for a third term. The US Constitution says that can't happen
item.description: President Donald Trump has just started his second term, his last one permitted under the U.S. Constitution. But he's already started talking about serving a third one. “There are methods which you can do it,” Trump insisted to NBC News in a telephone interview on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House abruptly fires career Justice Department prosecutors in latest norm-shattering move
item.description: The recent firings of career Justice Department lawyers by the White House is a sign of President Donald Trump’s tightening grip over the law enforcement agency known for its long tradition of political independence.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: The US is not ready for a nuclear showdown with China, key conservatives warn Trump
item.description: The United States is ill-prepared for a potential nuclear showdown with China, key thinkers with the Heritage Foundation are warning President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: DISTURBING CONTENT WARNING: Illegal accused of killing Georgia grandmother faces new disturbing charges
item.description: The illegal immigrant accused of killing an Atlanta-area grandmother has been indicted and now faces additional disturbing charges related to the brutal murder.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fire at New Mexico GOP headquarters under investigation as arson
item.description: A fire that damaged the entryway to the New Mexico Republican Party headquarters in Albuquerque is being investigated as arson.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New research reveals how EV, emissions mandates are inflating costs for gas-powered cars, utilities
item.description: The American Energy Institute released new research this week explaining how EV subsidies, combined with an emissions credit system, is causing automobile and utility costs to rise.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Protests grow, questions swirl about U of M international student detained by ICE
item.description: Protesters gathered at the University of Minnesota Twin Cities campus after an international graduate student was detained by immigration officials on Thursday. Since the incident, little information has been released by the university or law enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini, Matt Sepic, and Aleesa Kuznetsov, MPR News

item.title: Harris pleaded with Biden for speedy endorsement
item.description: Former Vice President Kamala Harris pleaded with former President Biden to endorse her White House bid on the same day as his historic move to end his 2024 reelection bid, according to exclusive excerpts from a forthcoming book.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Mayor Adams' lawyer urges ‘prompt resolution’ ahead of election deadline
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – With a filing deadline approaching in New York City’s upcoming mayoral election, Mayor Eric Adams’ lawyer has urged a judge to "promptly" issue a decision in the mayor’s bribery case. Petitions are due this week for candidates to appear on a party's primary ballot.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: The Latest: Trump to roll out new tariffs that he promises will free the US from foreign goods
item.description: President Donald Trump says Wednesday will be “Liberation Day” — when he plans to roll out a set of tariffs he promises will free the United States from foreign goods.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 6 things borrowers should know about federal student loans right now
item.description: NPR has spent the past few weeks catching up with student loan experts and asking the Trump administration for clarity on some of borrowers’ biggest questions.
item.dc:creator: Cory Turner, MPR News

item.title: Trump won't rule out military force to take Greenland
item.description: In an interview with NBC News, Trump refused to rule out military force to acquire Greenland. His remarks come a day after Vice President JD Vance's scaled back visit to the island.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Wisconsin's attorney general asks the state Supreme Court to stop Musk's $1 million payments
item.description: Wisconsin’s attorney general has asked the liberal-controlled state Supreme Court to stop billionaire Elon Musk from handing over $1 million checks to two voters. The request comes hours before President Donald Trump’s ally plans the giveaway at an evening rally in Green Bay.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump 'very angry' at Putin's remarks on Zelensky
item.description: The president threatened to slap a new tariff on Russia if it is at fault for stalling an end "to bloodshed.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Red Lake Nation leader condemns Trump orders during State of the Band Address
item.description: During the Red Lake Nation State of the Band Address Friday, chair Darrell Seki Jr., condemned President Donald Trump’s recent executive orders, saying they have created a crisis throughout Indian Country.
item.dc:creator: Mathew Holding Eagle III, MPR News

item.title: Florida considers bill to roll back child labor laws to fill jobs once held by undocumented migrants
item.description: Florida lawmakers are considering major changes to state child labor laws to address a potential shortage of migrant workers due to GOP-led immigration crackdowns, which would allow teens to work longer hours and earn lower wages.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, PIX 11

item.title: Most Florida voters approve of President Trump, Gov. DeSantis in office, new poll shows
item.description: A new poll shows that the majority of Florida voters approve of the work being done by President Donald Trump and Gov. Ron DeSantis.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US infrastructure improved with Biden-era spending but there's a long way to go
item.description: A report card on the upkeep of America's infrastructure gave it a “C” grade on Tuesday, up slightly from previous years.
item.dc:creator: TAMMY WEBBER and MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Live updates: Ratcliffe, Gabbard pressed on war plans Signal group chat
item.description: The Trump administration is scrambling to contain the fallout from a bombshell Atlantic article revealing its editor-in-chief had been mistakenly included in a Signal group chat involving national security adviser Mike Waltz, Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, intelligence leadership and other Trump ad
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, Fox 5
